title: Smoky Zesty Cold Egyptian Tamarind Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful cold tagine made with tamarind pulp, perfect for hot desert days. Serve on a wooden board for a truly authentic experience.
instructions: Smoke the tamarind pulp over low heat for 30 minutes to develop a rich, complex flavor.
Let the tamarind pulp cool to room temperature.
In a blender or food processor, combine the smoked tamarind pulp, olive oil, smoked paprika, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper. Blend until smooth.
Transfer the tamarind mixture to a bowl and stir in the Greek yogurt until well combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow the flavors to meld.
Stir in the chopped cilantro, lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, and chopped dates.
Serve the Smoky Zesty Cold Egyptian Tamarind Tagine chilled, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Arrange the tagine on a wooden board for an authentic presentation.
Garnish with additional chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
Serve with warm pita bread or crispy flatbread for a satisfying snack.
This dish is best served immediately, but it can be refrigerated for up to 24 hours.
To serve, stir in the chopped cilantro and lemon juice just before serving.
Garnish with additional chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
